title: Concept behind magclass
text: main idea behind magclass object data class always quite clear data organized. knowledge independent data allows write generic functions can treat various kinds data sets without knowing anything advance. magclass data class child class array, meaning based features. Contrary array magpie object always three dimensional, containing spatial information first dimension, temporal information second dimension everything else third dimension. However, limitation 3D-structure mean limited data 3 dimensions. Also data one -called “data dimension” (dimension neither temporal spatial) can handled: array three dimensions take one dimension exactly one element array reduced 2D object, whereas magclass object always remain 3D object. addition lot checks executed calculations magclass objects prevent unwanted things accidentally sum wrong numbers objects ordered differently: additional comfort safety bought performance meaning calculations resource consuming might want convert magclass object array, calculations (careful) convert back magclass object.
code: as.magpie(b) #> An object of class "magpie" #> , , NA = IndexA #>  #>      NA #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR     1     2     3 #>   CPA     4     5     6 #>   EUR     7     8     9 #>  #> , , NA = IndexB #>  #>      NA #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR    10    11    12 #>   CPA    13    14    15 #>   EUR    16    17    18 ar <- array(1:6, c(3, 2), list(c("bla", "blub", "ble"),                                c("up", "down"))) a <- as.magpie(ar) head(a) #> An object of class "magpie" #> , , NA = bla.up #>  #>      NA #> fake  [,1] #>   GLO    1 #>  #> , , NA = blub.up #>  #>      NA #> fake  [,1] #>   GLO    2  getItems(a, dim = 3) #> [1] "bla.up"    "blub.up"   "ble.up"    "bla.down"  "blub.down" #> [6] "ble.down"  getItems(a[, , "up"], dim = 3) #> [1] "bla.up"  "blub.up" "ble.up"  getItems(a[, , "up", drop = TRUE], dim = 3) #> [1] "bla"  "blub" "ble"  getItems(a, dim = 3, split = TRUE) #> $V1 #> [1] "bla"  "blub" "ble"  #>  #> $V2 #> [1] "up"   "down" c <- as.magpie(b) # change order of spatial components d <- c[3:1, , ]  # correct summation based on dimension names: c + d #> An object of class "magpie" #> , , data = IndexA #>  #>      year #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR     2     4     6 #>   CPA     8    10    12 #>   EUR    14    16    18 #>  #> , , data = IndexB #>  #>      year #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR    20    22    24 #>   CPA    26    28    30 #>   EUR    32    34    36  # wrong summation (dimension names ignored): as.array(c) + as.array(d) #> , , NA = IndexA #>  #>      NA #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR     8    10    12 #>   CPA     8    10    12 #>   EUR     8    10    12 #>  #> , , NA = IndexB #>  #>      NA #> fake  y2000 y2001 y2002 #>   AFR    26    28    30 #>   CPA    26    28    30 #>   EUR    26    28    30

text: origin object one name-column aim Can twofold: magpie object numeric value. start_year year convergence origin aim starts. set NULL first year aim used start_year end_year year convergence origin aim shall (nearly) reached. set NULL last year aim used end_year. direction NULL, "" "". NULL means normal convergence directions, "" convergence origin<aim, "" means convergence origin>aim type "smooth", "s", "linear" "decay". Describes function used convergence (x starts 0 ends 1): "linear" means linear interpolation. "s" s-curve starts origin start_year reaches aim precisely end_year. 50% convergence time, reaches middle two values. using function x^4/(0.07+x^4)*1.07). "smooth" conversion based function x^3/(0.1+x^3). "smooth" 90% convergence reached end year, full convergence approached x approaches infinity. "decay" conversion based function x / (par + x) * (par + 1). par parameter value convergence function; currently used type="decay"

Definition format characterized following features: file ending ".mif" file written ASCII format Entries separated ";", every line ends ";" file always contains header format header : Model;Scenario;Region;Variable;Unit;<ADDITIONAL_COLUMNS>;<YEARS>; first 5 entries always exist, <ADDITIONAL_COLUMNS> additional information can added optionally (e.g. "Description") <YEARS> years data delivered. <YEARS> always written 4 digit numbers. (unlikely) case year 1000 used number start 0, e.g. 0950. <ADDITIONAL_COLUMNS> can anything, rules moment can contain. However, strict rules naming columns. Allowed single names starting capital letter without special characters except "_" allowed. Examples: "Description" allowed, "Description" allowed, "More_Description" allowed, "123Description" allowed, "Description123" allowed. title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
code: write.magpie(   x,   file_name,   file_folder = "",   file_type = NULL,   append = FALSE,   comment = NULL,   comment.char = "*",   mode = NULL,   zname = "time",   ... )

title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
text: x magclass object. exception formats written via raster package (currently "asc", "grd" "tif") also accept RasterBrick objects previously created magclass object via .RasterBrick) file_name file name including file ending (wildcards supported). Optionally also full path can specified (instead splitting file_name file_folder) file_folder folder file written (alternatively can also specify full path file_name - wildcards supported) file_type Format data stored . file_type=NULL file ending file_name used format. See detailed description list available file types. Please aware file_name independent file_type choose , additional file ending added! append Decides whether existing file overwritten (FALSE) data added (TRUE). Append = TRUE works existing data can combined new data using mbind function comment Vector strings: Optional comment giving additional information data. different NULL overwrite content attr(x,"comment"). nc files unit can passed e.g. 'comment = "unit: kg"'. comment.char character: character vector length one containing single character empty string. Use "" turn interpretation comments altogether. mode File permissions file written 3-digit number (e.g. "777" means full access user, group , "750" means full access user, read access group acess anybody else). Set NULL system defaults used. Access codes identical codes used unix function chmod. zname name time variable raster files like nc, asc, grd tif ... additional arguments passed specific write functions. Check ?magclass::writeNC available arguments writing nc files.

title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
text: function supports writing following file types: "cs2" new standard format cellular data without header first columns (year,regiospatial) (regiospatial) "cs2b" identical "cs2" except suppress data name 1 element data dimension. "csv" standard format regional data without header first columns (year,region,cellnumber) (region,cellnumber) "cs3" another csv format specifically designed multidimensional data usage GAMS. "cs4" alternative multidimensional format compatible GAMS, contrast cs3 can also handle sparse data "csvr", "cs2r", "cs3r", "cs4r" formats ones previously explained difference REMIND compatible format "cs5" generalized version cs4 "rds" R-default format storing R objects "m" (magpie) "mz" (magpie zipped) new formats developed allow less storage intensive management MAgPIE-data. difference formats .mz gzipped whereas .m compressed. .mz needs less memory, whereas .m might higher compatibility languages "asc" ASCII grid format. can applied gridded data writes one file per year per data column. case one year data column supplied several files written structure filename_year_datacolumn.asc "tif"  GEOtiff format gridded data. "grd" native raster format gridded data. "nc" netCDF format gridded data. Check ?magclass::writeNC details nc files written.

title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
text:
binary MAgPIE formats .m .mz following content/structure (care want implement read.magpie/write.magpie functions languages):  [ FileFormatVersion | Current file format version number (currently 6) | integer | 2 Byte ]  [ nchar_comment | Number character bytes file comment | integer | 4 Byte ]  [ nbyte_metadata | Number bytes serialized metadata (currently = 0) | integer | 4 Byte ]  [ nchar_sets | Number characters bytes regionnames + 2 delimiter | integer | 2 Byte]  [ nyears | Number years | integer | 2 Byte ] [ yearList | years dataset (0, year present) | integer | 2*nyears Byte ]  [ ncells | Number cells | integer | 4 Byte ] [ nchar_cell | Number characters bytes regionnames + (nreg-1) delimiters | integer | 4 Byte ]  [ cells | Cell names saved cell1\cell2 (
 delimiter) | character | 1*nchar_cell Byte ]  [ nelem | Total number data elements | integer | 4 Byte ]  [ nchar_data | Number char. bytes datanames + (ndata - 1) delimiters | integer | 4 Byte ]  [ datanames | Names saved format data1
data2 (
 del.) | character | 1*nchar_data Byte ]  [ data | Data MAgPIE array vectorized form | numeric | 4*nelem Byte ]  [ comment | Comment additional information data | character | 1*nchar_comment Byte ]  [ sets | Set names 
 delimiter | character | 1*nchar_sets Byte]  [ metadata | serialized metadata information (currently use) | bytes | 1*nbyte_metadata Byte]

title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
text: Jan Philipp Dietrich, Stephen Bi, Florian Humpenoeder, Pascal Sauer

title: Write MAgPIE-object to file — write.magpie
code: pop <- maxample("pop") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".mz") write.magpie(pop, path) pop2 <- read.magpie(path)
